content_en:
300 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

The highest among those in knowing Allah is the one who knows Him through His words; he knows the Lord has all the attributes of perfection and all the characteristics of glory, far above having a parallel, and free from any defects and faults. He has every good name and every perfect attribute, the doer of all that He intends, above all matters and accompanies every matter, capable of every matter and builds every matter, absolute master, speaks with His Divine words, greater than every matter and more beautiful than anything, the most merciful among those who show mercy, the most powerful among those who have power, and the most wise among those who have wisdom. The Qur'an was revealed to inform His servants about Him, the Way that leads to Him, and the conditions of those who followed in His way after knowing Him.

A Precious Gem: Allah will not Change the Condition of a People until They Change Themselves

Quite often the servant of Allah is granted abundant blessings but he becomes bored and longs to change it for another which he claims is better. In fact, Allah, the Merciful does not deprive him of this blessing, and He excuses him for his ignorance and bad choice until the servant is unable to bear the blessing, feels discontent, and complains about it. Then Allah will take it away from him. When he gets what he wished for and sees the great difference between what he had before and what he has now, he is filled with worry and regret and he wishes to have what he had before. If Allah wishes good for His servant, He would make him see that whatever blessings he now has is from Allah and He will show him that Allah is pleased with him, and the servant would praise Him. If he is deceived by his soul to change this blessing, he would ask Allah for guidance.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 301

There is nothing more harmful to the servant than becoming bored from the blessings of Allah; as he neither sees them as a blessing, praises Allah for them, nor is happy with them but he becomes bored, complains, and considers them as a means of distress. He does not think that these things are from the greatest blessings of Allah. The majority of people are opposed to the blessings of Allah. They do not feel the blessings of Allah, and moreover, they exert their effort to drive them away because of their ignorance and injustice. How often is a blessing bestowed on a person while he is exerting his effort to drive it away and how often does he actually receive it while he is pushing it away, simply because of his ignorance and messes Allah says,

oe 7h eZ coe

which means, "That is so because Allah will never change a grace which He has bestowed on a people until they change what is in their ownselves." (Al-Anfal, 8:53)

And He, the Almighty says,

which means, "Allah will not change the good condition of a people as long as they do not change their state of goodness themselves (by committing sins and by being ungrateful and disobedient to Allah)." (Ar-Ra'd, 13:11)

What can be worse than the enmity of a servant toward the blessings he has received? In so doing, he supports his enemy against himself. His enemy arouses fire in his blessings and he increases the fire unawares. He enables his enemy to light the fire and then he helps his own enemy to blow on it until it becomes strong. Finally, he seeks help against the fire and blames fate.

302 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

Chapter: The Beauty of Allah, the Exalted

The most precious kind of knowledge e is to know Allah, the Exalted through His beauty. This is granted to the truly pious among His creatures, while others know Him through one of His attributes, while those who know Him the best know Him through His perfection, glory, and beauty. There is nothing like Him in His attributes. If we assume that all creatures are beautiful and we think of the most beautiful one we know, and compare their inner and outer beauty to that of Allah, the Exalted, it will be like we are comparing the light of a candle to the sun. Surely, if He withdraws the veil from His face, the splendor of His countenance would consume His creation. The beauty that is contained in the inner and outer elements of this worldly life and the Hereafter comes from the effect of His creation. What then, is the extent of beauty of the Creator?

He has all glory, power, charity, generosity, knowledge, and favor. With the light of His face, all darkness disappears. As the Prophet (peace be upon him) said in supplication of At-Taif, "| seek refuge with Your Face that darkness will go and without which both the affairs of this life and the Hereafter will go astray."

‘Abdullah bin Mas'td (may Allah be pleased with him) said, "Allah does not have either night or day: the light of the Heavens and the earth is from the light of His face." Allah, the Exalted is the light of the heavens and the earth and on the Day of Resurrection when Allah will judge mankind, the earth will shine from His light. From among the names of Allah is “the most Graceful." It was recorded in the sahih that the

AL-FAWA'D : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 303

The beauty of Allah is on four levels: the beauty of self, the beauty of His attributes, the beauty of His deeds, and the beauty of His names. All His names are beautiful, His attributes are all attributes of perfection, and His deeds are all wise, just, and merciful, and He considers the benefit of man. Only Allah knows the beauty of self and what it is like, and His creatures only have some definitions that He bestowed upon some of His servants that He honored. This beauty is preserved away from others and is covered by a veil and a lower garment, as the Messenger (peace be upon him) said, "Pride is my cloak and majesty is my lower garment.” And because pride is wider and greater, it deserves the name of cloak, as Allah is the Greatest and the Most High.

ion ‘Abbas said, "Allah covered His self with attributes and covered His attributes with deeds. What do you think about a beauty that is covered by the attributes of perfection and of glory and loftiness?"

From this some other meanings of beauty can be understood. The servant achieves progress from knowing the deeds and then the attributes, and from knowing the attributes to knowing the self. If he saw something among the beauty of deeds, he would reach the beauty of attributes and then he reaches the beauty of the self.

So all praise and thanks are due to Allah, the Exalted , and none of His creation can praise Him as He deserves, but only as He praises Himself and He deserves to be worshiped, loved, and praised. He loves His attributes and deeds as He loves His self. All of His deeds are beautiful and precious and there is nothing hateful among His deeds. There is nothing in

304 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

the universe that is loved and praised except Allah, the Exalted. And whatever is loved other than Him, if love is for the sake of Allah, this love is lawful otherwise it is not. And this is the reality of Lordship. s The true Lord is the one that is loved and praised. What then if we add to that His charity, favor, mercy, forgiveness, and piety?

The servant should know that there is no god but Allah, so he would love and praise Him for His self and for His perfection and should know that there is no one that grants secret and public favors and blessings except Him, so he would love Him for His compassion and blessings. As there is nothing similar to Him, loving Him is not like any kind of love; loving with submission is the kind of worship that Allah created all things for. It is the utmost love with the utmost humility and it cannot be shown except to Allah. Indeed to show it to other than Him is polytheism that Allah does not forgive if the doer dies in that state.

Praising Him includes two matters: being informed about His praiseworthy acts and His attributes of perfection, and loving Him because of them. If someone informs another person about their praiseworthy acts without loving him, he is not praising him. And whoever loves him without informing others about his praiseworthy acts, he is not praising him until he combines the two matters. Allah, the Exalted praises Himself by Himself and praises Himself by the tongues of those who praise Him from among the angels, the Prophets, the Messengers, and the believers. They praise Him according to His wish and by His permission. He is the one, Who enables them to praise Him, and ali things are done with His permission. All blessings begin and end with Him; they begin with His praise and end with His praise. He inspires His servant to repent and is happy with it while it is from His favor and generosity and He inspires His servant to obey, helps him to do that, and rewards him for it! He is in no need of anything but all things are in deep need of

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 305

Him at all times. The servant needs Him to help him to achieve his aims. Whatever is not permitted by Him will never be and whatever is not done for His sake is useless.

Chapter: Allah is Full of Grace and He loves Grace
content_id:
300 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-kata Bijak

Yang paling tinggi di antara mereka yang mengenal Allah adalah orang yang mengenal-Nya melalui firman-Nya; ia mengetahui bahwa Rabb memiliki semua sifat kesempurnaan dan semua karakteristik kemuliaan, jauh dari memiliki tandingan, dan bebas dari segala kekurangan dan cacat. Dia memiliki setiap nama yang baik dan setiap sifat yang sempurna, Pelaku segala yang Dia kehendaki, di atas segala urusan dan menyertai setiap urusan, berkuasa atas segala urusan dan membangun setiap urusan, Penguasa mutlak, berbicara dengan firman-firman Ilahi-Nya, lebih agung dari segala urusan dan lebih indah dari segala sesuatu, Yang Paling Penyayang di antara mereka yang menunjukkan kasih sayang, Yang Paling Berkuasa di antara mereka yang memiliki kekuasaan, dan Yang Paling Bijaksana di antara mereka yang memiliki hikmah. Al-Quran diturunkan untuk memberitahu hamba-hamba-Nya tentang Dia, Jalan yang menuju kepada-Nya, dan keadaan mereka yang mengikuti jalan-Nya setelah mengenal-Nya.

Permata Berharga: Allah Tidak Akan Mengubah Keadaan Suatu Kaum Hingga Mereka Mengubah Diri Mereka Sendiri

Cukup sering hamba Allah diberikan nikmat yang melimpah tetapi ia menjadi bosan dan ingin mengubahnya dengan yang lain yang ia klaim lebih baik. Padahal, Allah Yang Maha Penyayang tidak mencabut nikmat ini darinya, dan Dia memaafkannya atas ketidaktahuan dan pilihan buruknya hingga hamba itu tidak mampu lagi menanggung nikmat tersebut, merasa tidak puas, dan mengeluhkannya. Maka Allah akan mencabutnya darinya. Ketika ia mendapatkan apa yang ia inginkan dan melihat perbedaan besar antara apa yang ia miliki sebelumnya dan apa yang ia miliki sekarang, ia dipenuhi kekhawatiran dan penyesalan dan berharap mendapatkan kembali apa yang ia miliki sebelumnya. Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi hamba-Nya, Dia akan membuatnya melihat bahwa nikmat apa pun yang ia miliki sekarang adalah dari Allah dan Dia akan menunjukkan kepadanya bahwa Allah ridha kepadanya, dan hamba itu akan memuji-Nya. Jika ia tertipu oleh jiwanya untuk mengubah nikmat ini, ia akan memohon petunjuk kepada Allah.

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-kata Bijak * 301

Tidak ada yang lebih berbahaya bagi seorang hamba daripada menjadi bosan terhadap nikmat-nikmat Allah; karena ia tidak melihatnya sebagai nikmat, tidak memuji Allah karenanya, dan tidak bahagia dengannya, tetapi ia menjadi bosan, mengeluh, dan menganggapnya sebagai sumber kesusahan. Ia tidak berpikir bahwa hal-hal ini termasuk nikmat Allah yang terbesar. Mayoritas manusia menentang nikmat-nikmat Allah. Mereka tidak merasakan nikmat-nikmat Allah, bahkan mereka mengerahkan usaha mereka untuk mengusirnya karena ketidaktahuan dan kezaliman mereka. Betapa sering nikmat diberikan kepada seseorang sementara ia mengerahkan usahanya untuk mengusirnya dan betapa sering ia benar-benar menerimanya sementara ia mendorongnya pergi, semata-mata karena ketidaktahuan dan kekacauannya. Allah berfirman,

oe 7h eZ coe

yang artinya, "Yang demikian itu karena sesungguhnya Allah tidak akan mengubah suatu nikmat yang telah diberikan-Nya kepada suatu kaum, hingga kaum itu mengubah apa yang ada pada diri mereka sendiri." (Al-Anfal, 8:53)

Dan Dia Yang Maha Kuasa berfirman,

yang artinya, "Allah tidak akan mengubah keadaan baik suatu kaum selama mereka tidak mengubah keadaan kebaikan mereka sendiri (dengan melakukan dosa-dosa dan dengan bersikap tidak bersyukur serta durhaka kepada Allah)." (Ar-Ra'd, 13:11)

Apa yang bisa lebih buruk daripada permusuhan seorang hamba terhadap nikmat yang telah ia terima? Dengan melakukan itu, ia mendukung musuhnya melawan dirinya sendiri. Musuhnya menyalakan api pada nikmat-nikmatnya dan ia menambah api itu tanpa disadari. Ia memungkinkan musuhnya menyalakan api dan kemudian ia membantu musuhnya sendiri meniupnya hingga menjadi kuat. Akhirnya, ia memohon pertolongan dari api itu dan menyalahkan takdir.

302 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-kata Bijak

Bab: Keindahan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi

Jenis pengetahuan yang paling berharga adalah mengenal Allah Yang Maha Tinggi melalui keindahan-Nya. Ini diberikan kepada orang-orang yang benar-benar bertaqwa di antara makhluk-Nya, sementara yang lain mengenal-Nya melalui salah satu sifat-Nya, sedangkan mereka yang paling mengenal-Nya mengenal-Nya melalui kesempurnaan, keagungan, dan keindahan-Nya. Tidak ada sesuatu pun yang serupa dengan-Nya dalam sifat-sifat-Nya. Jika kita berandai-andai bahwa seluruh makhluk itu indah dan kita membayangkan yang paling indah yang kita ketahui, lalu membandingkan keindahan lahir dan batin mereka dengan keindahan Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, itu akan seperti kita membandingkan cahaya lilin dengan matahari. Sungguh, jika Dia menyingkap tabir dari wajah-Nya, cahaya keagungan wajah-Nya akan menghancurkan seluruh ciptaan-Nya. Keindahan yang terkandung dalam unsur-unsur lahir dan batin kehidupan dunia ini dan akhirat berasal dari pengaruh ciptaan-Nya. Lalu, sejauh mana keindahan Sang Pencipta?

Dia memiliki segala kemuliaan, kekuasaan, kedermawanan, kemurahan, ilmu, dan karunia. Dengan cahaya wajah-Nya, semua kegelapan lenyap. Sebagaimana Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda dalam du'a di Thaif, "Aku berlindung dengan wajah-Mu yang dengannya kegelapan akan pergi dan tanpanya urusan kehidupan dunia dan akhirat akan tersesat."

'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata, "Allah tidak memiliki malam atau siang: cahaya langit dan bumi berasal dari cahaya wajah-Nya." Allah Yang Maha Tinggi adalah cahaya langit dan bumi dan pada Hari Kiamat ketika Allah akan mengadili manusia, bumi akan bersinar dari cahaya-Nya. Di antara nama-nama Allah adalah "Yang Maha Indah." Telah diriwayatkan dalam hadith shahih bahwa

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-kata Bijak * 303

Keindahan Allah berada pada empat tingkatan: keindahan Dzat, keindahan sifat-sifat-Nya, keindahan perbuatan-perbuatan-Nya, dan keindahan nama-nama-Nya. Semua nama-Nya indah, sifat-sifat-Nya semuanya adalah sifat kesempurnaan, dan perbuatan-perbuatan-Nya semuanya bijaksana, adil, dan penuh rahmat, dan Dia mempertimbangkan kemaslahatan manusia. Hanya Allah yang mengetahui keindahan Dzat dan bagaimana hakikatnya, dan makhluk-Nya hanya memiliki sebagian definisi yang Dia berikan kepada sebagian hamba-Nya yang Dia muliakan. Keindahan ini tersimpan dari yang lain dan tertutup oleh tabir dan pakaian bawah, sebagaimana Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda, "Keangkuhan adalah jubah-Ku dan keagungan adalah pakaian bawah-Ku." Dan karena keangkuhan lebih luas dan lebih besar, ia layak mendapat sebutan jubah, sebagaimana Allah adalah Yang Maha Besar dan Yang Maha Tinggi.

Ibnu 'Abbas berkata, "Allah menutupi Dzat-Nya dengan sifat-sifat dan menutupi sifat-sifat-Nya dengan perbuatan-perbuatan. Apa pendapatmu tentang keindahan yang tertutup oleh sifat-sifat kesempurnaan, keagungan, dan keluhuran?"

Dari sini beberapa makna keindahan lainnya dapat dipahami. Seorang hamba mencapai kemajuan dari mengenal perbuatan-perbuatan lalu sifat-sifat, dan dari mengenal sifat-sifat menuju mengenal Dzat. Jika ia melihat sesuatu dari keindahan perbuatan, ia akan sampai pada keindahan sifat dan kemudian ia sampai pada keindahan Dzat.

Maka segala puji dan syukur hanya milik Allah Yang Maha Tinggi, dan tidak ada seorang pun dari makhluk-Nya yang dapat memuji-Nya sebagaimana Dia layak dipuji, melainkan sebagaimana Dia memuji diri-Nya sendiri dan Dia layak untuk disembah, dicintai, dan dipuji. Dia mencintai sifat-sifat dan perbuatan-perbuatan-Nya sebagaimana Dia mencintai Dzat-Nya. Semua perbuatan-Nya indah dan mulia dan tidak ada yang dibenci di antara perbuatan-perbuatan-Nya. Tidak ada sesuatu pun di

304 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-kata Bijak

alam semesta yang dicintai dan dipuji selain Allah Yang Maha Tinggi. Dan apa pun yang dicintai selain-Nya, jika cinta itu karena Allah, maka cinta ini halal, jika tidak maka tidak. Dan inilah hakikat Rububiyyah. Rabb yang sejati adalah Yang dicintai dan dipuji. Lalu bagaimana jika kita menambahkan kepada itu kedermawanan-Nya, karunia-Nya, rahmat-Nya, ampunan-Nya, dan ketaqwaan-Nya?

Seorang hamba hendaknya mengetahui bahwa tidak ada ilah selain Allah, maka ia akan mencintai dan memuji-Nya karena Dzat-Nya dan karena kesempurnaan-Nya dan hendaknya mengetahui bahwa tidak ada yang memberikan nikmat-nikmat tersembunyi dan tampak kecuali Dia, maka ia akan mencintai-Nya karena kasih sayang dan nikmat-nikmat-Nya. Karena tidak ada sesuatu pun yang serupa dengan-Nya, mencintai-Nya tidak seperti cinta apa pun; mencintai dengan ketundukan adalah jenis ibadah yang Allah ciptakan segala sesuatu untuknya. Ia adalah cinta yang paling tinggi dengan kerendahan hati yang paling dalam dan tidak boleh ditunjukkan kecuali kepada Allah. Sesungguhnya menunjukkannya kepada selain-Nya adalah shirk yang tidak diampuni Allah jika pelakunya meninggal dalam keadaan tersebut.

Memuji-Nya mencakup dua hal: memberitahukan tentang perbuatan-perbuatan terpuji-Nya dan sifat-sifat kesempurnaan-Nya, dan mencintai-Nya karenanya. Jika seseorang memberitahu orang lain tentang perbuatan-perbuatan terpuji seseorang tanpa mencintainya, ia tidak sedang memujinya. Dan siapa pun yang mencintainya tanpa memberitahu orang lain tentang perbuatan-perbuatan terpujinya, ia tidak sedang memujinya hingga ia menggabungkan kedua hal tersebut. Allah Yang Maha Tinggi memuji diri-Nya dengan diri-Nya sendiri dan memuji diri-Nya dengan lisan orang-orang yang memuji-Nya dari kalangan para malaikat, para Nabi, para Rasul, dan orang-orang beriman. Mereka memuji-Nya sesuai dengan kehendak-Nya dan dengan izin-Nya. Dialah Yang memampukan mereka untuk memuji-Nya, dan segala sesuatu dilakukan dengan izin-Nya. Semua nikmat bermula dan berakhir dengan-Nya; bermula dengan pujian-Nya dan berakhir dengan pujian-Nya. Dia mengilhami hamba-Nya untuk bertaubat dan bahagia dengannya sementara itu berasal dari karunia dan kemurahan-Nya dan Dia mengilhami hamba-Nya untuk taat, membantunya melakukan itu, dan memberinya pahala karenanya! Dia tidak membutuhkan apa pun tetapi segala sesuatu sangat membutuhkan

AL-FAWA'ID : Kumpulan Kata-kata Bijak * 305

kepada-Nya setiap saat. Hamba membutuhkan-Nya untuk membantunya mencapai tujuannya. Apa pun yang tidak diizinkan oleh-Nya tidak akan pernah terjadi dan apa pun yang tidak dilakukan demi-Nya adalah sia-sia.

Bab: Allah Maha Penuh Karunia dan Dia Mencintai Karunia
content_ar:
300 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

The highest among those in knowing الله is the one who knows Him through His words; he knows the Lord has all the attributes of perfection and all the characteristics of glory, far above having a parallel, and free from any defects and faults. He has every good name and every perfect attribute, the doer of all that He intends, above all matters and accompanies every matter, capable of every matter and builds every matter, absolute master, speaks with His Divine words, greater than every matter and more beautiful than anything, the most merciful among those who show mercy, the most powerful among those who have power, and the most wise among those who have wisdom. The Qur'an was revealed to inform His servants about Him, the Way that leads to Him, and the conditions of those who followed in His way after knowing Him.

A Precious Gem: الله will not Change the Condition of a People until They Change Themselves

Quite often the servant of الله is granted abundant blessings but he becomes bored and longs to change it for another which he claims is better. In fact, الله, the Merciful does not deprive him of this blessing, and He excuses him for his ignorance and bad choice until the servant is unable to bear the blessing, feels discontent, and complains about it. Then الله will take it away from him. When he gets what he wished for and sees the great difference between what he had before and what he has now, he is filled with worry and regret and he wishes to have what he had before. If الله wishes good for His servant, He would make him see that whatever blessings he now has is from الله and He will show him that الله is pleased with him, and the servant would praise Him. If he is deceived by his soul to change this blessing, he would ask الله for guidance.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 301

There is nothing more harmful to the servant than becoming bored from the blessings of الله; as he neither sees them as a blessing, praises الله for them, nor is happy with them but he becomes bored, complains, and considers them as a means of distress. He does not think that these things are from the greatest blessings of الله. The majority of people are opposed to the blessings of الله. They do not feel the blessings of الله, and moreover, they exert their effort to drive them away because of their ignorance and injustice. How often is a blessing bestowed on a person while he is exerting his effort to drive it away and how often does he actually receive it while he is pushing it away, simply because of his ignorance and messes الله says,

oe 7h eZ coe

which means, "That is so because الله will never change a grace which He has bestowed on a people until they change what is in their ownselves." (Al-Anfal, 8:53)

And He, the Almighty says,

which means, "الله will not change the good condition of a people as long as they do not
